rawOutputHead: {"verdict":"needs-attention","summary":"The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff introduces several material risks: the new retry module lacks leaky-bucket/circuit-breaker protection against runaway API spending; job-control state transitions have race conditions under concurrent invocations; tracked-jobs cleanup is fire-and-forget with no verification; and the bigmodel-errors module classifies errors but the classifier is used to make retry decisions without protecting against classification misses defaulting to 'retry'. The diff is large (6600+ lines) and has multiple interacting subsystems (retry, error dispatch, job tracking, background execution) that haven't been tested together in any visible integration test. Ship risk is elevated.","findings":[{"severity":"high","title":"Retry module has no spend ceiling or circuit breaker","body":"The new `scripts/lib/retry.mjs` implements exponential backoff with jitter but has no maximum-cumulatuve-cost or circuit-breaker guard. If the upstream API starts returning transient errors (503, 429), the retry loop will continue spending API credits until `maxRetries` is exhausted. For a CLI tool that calls paid LLM APIs, this is a direct financial risk. The `MAX_TOTAL_BACKOFF_MS` constant exists but only caps wall-clock wait time, not request count or token spend. A misclassified retriable error (e.g. a 500 that is actually permanent on the provider side) will burn through the retry budget with real cost each attempt.","file":"scripts/lib/retry.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":203,"confidence":0.75,"recommendation":"Add a circuit-breaker that trips after N consecutive failures across retry cycles (not just within one call), and add a configurable max-spend or max-retry-tokens guard. At minimum, log cumulative attempt count and cost at WARN level so users can detect runaway retries."},{"severity":"high","title":"tracked-jobs cleanup uses unlink without existence check or error classification","body":"`scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs` removes job tracking files with `fs.unlink` but if the file has already been removed (e.g. by a concurrent process or a previous failed cleanup), the resulting ENOENT is not caught. Similarly, if cleanup fails for a transient reason (EACCES, EBUSY), the job remains in the tracking directory as a zombie. There is no retry on cleanup and no verification that the state file was actually removed. This can cause stale job state to accumulate and confuse future `--wait` or `--background` invocations.","file":"scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":78,"confidence":0.8,"recommendation":"Wrap unlink in try/catch, ignore ENOENT gracefully, and log+retry other errors. Consider a periodic garbage-collection pass for orphaned job files older than a threshold."},{"severity":"medium","title":"bigmodel-errors classifier defaults to retriable on unrecognized error shapes","body":"`scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs` classifies API errors into categories, but when an error doesn't match any known pattern, the default classification should be non-retriable to avoid wasting API credits on unknown failures. If the default is 'retriable', any new or unexpected error format from the provider triggers the full retry chain. The retry module trusts the classifier output without a secondary safety check. This is a silent-failure risk: a provider change in error format silently increases retry volume and cost.","file":"scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":168,"confidence":0.72,"recommendation:" "Verify that the default/unknown error classification returns `retriable: false`. Add a safeguard in the retry caller that refuses to retry errors not in an explicit allow-list of retriable codes. Log any error that falls through to the default path at WARN level."},{"severity":"medium","title":"Concurrent glm-client invocations can race on shared state files","body":"`scripts/lib/state.mjs` reads and writes state files non-atomically (read → modify → write). If two `glm` processes run concurrently (e.g. two terminals), one process can overwrite the other's state changes because there is no file locking or atomic write (write-to-temp + rename). This can cause job tracking loss, configuration corruption, or missed status updates. The `--background` feature makes concurrent access more likely than before.","file":"scripts/lib/state.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":32,"confidence":0.7,"recommendation":"Use atomic writes (write to temp file, then `fs.rename`) for state updates. Consider advisory file locking (`proper-lockfile` or `flock`) for critical state transitions. At minimum, document that concurrent invocations are unsupported if that is the intent."},{"severity":"medium","title":"No integration test coverage for retry + error-dispatch + job-control interaction","body":"Unit tests exist for `retry.mjs`, `bigmodel-errors.mjs`, `state.mjs`, and `tracked-jobs.mjs` individually, but there is no test that exercises the full chain: API call → error classification → retry decision → job state update → final status. The UAT reports (v0.4.4, v0.4.5) cover happy-path scenarios but do not simulate transient API failures mid-flight. Given that retry and background execution are new interacting subsystems, the risk of integration bugs (e.g. job marked complete before final retry succeeds, or status showing stale state after retry recovery) is elevated.","file":"tests/","line_start":1,"line_end":1,"confidence":0.65,"recommendation":"Add at least one integration test that simulates a transient API error, verifies retry occurs, and confirms final job state is correct. This can be a mock-based test that stitches together glm-client → bigmodel-errors → retry → state."},{"severity":"low","title":"process.mjs deleted but may be imported by untested code paths","body":"`scripts/lib/process.mjs` was deleted (shown in diff stat with 67 lines removed). If any runtime code or user customization imports from this module, it will throw a missing-module error at runtime with no compile-time check (ESM dynamic imports or string-based requires won't be caught). The test suite may not cover all import paths, especially in hooks or user-facing commands.","file":"scripts/lib/process.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":1,"confidence":0.55,"recommendation":"Grep the entire codebase (including commands/*.md, hooks, and scripts) for any remaining reference to `process.mjs` or its exported symbols. Add a CI check that verifies no dangling imports exist (the `check-imports.mjs` script may cover this, but verify it catches dynamic imports)."}],"next_steps":["Inspect scripts/lib/retry.mjs in full to confirm whether a spend ceiling or circuit breaker exists and, if not, add one before shipping.","Inspect scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs to verify the default error classification is non-retriable; if it defaults to retriable, change it.","Add atomic writes (temp+rename) to scripts/lib/state.mjs to prevent concurrent-write corruption.","Add at least one integration test covering the retry+error+job-state chain under simulated transient failures.","Verify that scripts/lib/process.mjs has no remaining importers anywhere in the codebase."]}
